# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that accepts build parameters from users and executes them as shell commands. Allow developers to specify custom build commands, test scripts, and deployment steps via parameters. This provides maximum flexibility for different project types.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    options {
        disableConcurrentBuilds()
    }

    parameters {
        string(name: 'BUILD_TYPE', description: 'Type of build (e.g., release, debug)')
        booleanParam(name: 'RUN_TEST', description: 'Run unit tests?')
        booleanParam(name: 'DEPLOY_TO_PROD', description: 'Deploy to production environment?')
        string(name: 'CUSTOM_BUILD_CMD', description: 'Custom build command (optional)')
        string(name: 'CUSTOM_DEPLOY_SCRIPT', description: 'Custom deployment script (optional)')
    }

    stages {
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                sh "echo Building $BUILD_TYPE..."
                if (params.CUSTOM_BUILD_CMD) {
                    sh "$CUSTOM_BUILD_CMD"
                } else {
                    sh "./build.sh -t ${params.BUILD_TYPE}"
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Test') {
            when {
                expression { params.RUN_TEST == true }
            }
            steps {
                sh "echo Running unit tests..."
                if (params.CUSTOM_DEPLOY_SCRIPT) {
                    sh "$CUSTOM_DEPLOY_SCRIPT"
                } else {
                    sh "./run-tests.sh"
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Deploy') {
            when {
                expression { params.DEPLOY_TO_PROD == true }
            }
            steps {
                sh "echo Deploying to production..."
                if (params.CUSTOM_DEPLOY_SCRIPT) {
                    sh "$CUSTOM_DEPLOY_SCRIPT"
                } else {
                    sh "./deploy-to-prod.sh"
                }
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        always {
            echo 'Build completed.'
        }
    }
}
